raw_consumer_statement:
To whom it may concern, I am submitting a regulatory complaint against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) regarding an unresolved dispute with the merchant XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX in the amount of {$640.00}, charged on XX/XX/year>, to my account ending in XXXX. 

The dispute was initiated under Ref. XXXX XXXXXXXX using the dispute reason : Cancelled/ Returned/ Refused Delivery for Goods or Services/ Expected credit not received. 

I have provided American Express with sufficient documentation showing that a refund was both warranted and promised. The car rental booking, scheduled for XX/XX/year> in XXXX, was cancelled in advance. Despite my repeated attempts to contact XXXX to confirm the cancellation, I received no timely response. Which the merchant has yet to provide written, which is a breach of contract. 

On the date of the scheduled booking, I was informed by a XXXX employee to escalate the matter to corporate, at which time they acknowledged that a refund was still due. This acknowledgment is reflected in the attached documentation. 

Furthermore, there is a clear discrepancy between XXXX  advertised no-hassle full refund policy and the terms and conditions they have attempted to enforce in this case. I also provided American Express with additional evidence referencing applicable consumer protection laws under XXXX, XXXX regulations, as well as U.S. federal regulations governing contractual and consumer rights ; both of which apply to American Express operations. 


Please find all supporting documentation attached for your review. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
